title: Add backpressure to a push stream
description: Bound per-connection buffers and define a slow-consumer policy so server memory stays flat under load.
body: Steps: 1. Give each connection a bounded outbound queue. 2. Measure write latency / queue depth per connection. 3. Define the slow-consumer policy: block the producer, coalesce to latest-state, or disconnect with resync. 4. Apply it when the queue crosses a high-water mark. 5. Emit a metric on drops/disconnects. Rollback point: revert to a larger but still bounded buffer. Validation: a slow-consumer load test shows server memory flat, the slow client coalesced or shed, and fast clients unaffected.
